unavailable_reason: No stored pass/total artifact exists. There is NO junit/pytest result file, no test_report json, and no stored stdout of scripts/run_tests.sh on disk. The brief instructs not to run a heavy new suite, so a fresh full-suite pass/total was not generated.

structural_inventory.source: static count via ripgrep over HCIE_SYSTEM_BACKEND_FINAL/02_tests (pattern '^\s*(async\s+)?def test_')
structural_inventory.caveat: This is the count of declared test functions, NOT executed scenarios and NOT a pass count.

last_host_pytest_cache.NOT_a_valid_pass_total: These 38 are HOST-environment collection/import failures, not functional scenario failures. scripts/run_coverage.sh states explicitly: 'only the UNIT tests run cleanly on the host. The integration/behavioral/research suites ... need the running stack (postgres/redis/kafka via docker hostnames)'. On the host those suites fail to import (e.g. test_k12_transfer_docker, test_c2_3_1_deterministic_replay, docker-dependent cases) -> the lastfailed list reflects missing docker network, not broken scenarios. This cache is therefore NOT a trustworthy pass/total.

coverage_artifact.note: Coverage exists (36.4% line coverage) but coverage != functional pass/total. This is the host unit-test coverage; the isolated full-stack run (scripts/run_tests.sh) is what exercises unified_brain etc.

isolated_stack_runner_recipe_not_execute.what_it_does: brings up an isolated pg/redis/kafka stack (docker-compose.test.yml), copies live SCHEMA (no data), runs pytest+coverage inside the api image (so core.* resolves and the brain wires its real stores), default scope = 00_unit 01_integration 02_behavioral, deterministic mode ENABLE_DETERMINISTIC_MODE=true seed=42.
isolated_stack_runner_recipe_not_execute.status: NOT RUN (analysis-only; running it spins a stack and is the heavy suite the brief said to avoid). A faithful pass/total requires executing this against the isolated stack.

honest_verdict: Functional scenarios pass/total = UNAVAILABLE as a stored, trustworthy number. Disk holds: 614 declared test functions across 124 files (structural), 36.4% line coverage (coverage.xml), and a host pytest cache whose 38 'failures' are docker-network collection errors, not scenario failures. To fill 4.1.2.a, run scripts/run_tests.sh once on the isolated stack and record its final '=== N passed, M failed ===' line.
